title: The Time Zone Disaster
content: I once scheduled a call for '9 AM' without specifying the time zone. Three people showed up at different times. Now I put the time zone in EVERY calendar invite. Every single one. Paranoid? Maybe. But effective.
extended_content: One person in London, one in New York, one in LA. Each showed up at 9 AM their time. I had to apologize to everyone, reschedule the whole thing, and explain to my boss why the meeting never happened. That was the day I learned: specificity isn't paranoia, it's professionalism. Now I include the timezone even for meetings with people in the same building. You never know who might forward that invite.
when_to_tell: when helping with international scheduling or calendar invites
